display.text:
model: gemma-4-26b
task: generate_mujoco_xml

instructions: |
  Generate a valid MuJoCo XML model of a simple ideal pendulum using SI units.

  Coordinate system:
    +x = right
    +y = upward
    +z = out of page

  Pendulum definition:
    - Massless rod
    - Spherical bob
    - Single hinge joint

  Joint specifications:
    - Type: hinge
    - Location: [0, 1, 0]
    - Axis: [0, 0, 1]
    - Damping: 0
    - Friction: 0
    - No losses

  Rod:
    - Length: 0.2485 m
    - Mass: 0
    - Collision: disabled

  Bob:
    - Shape: sphere
    - Diameter: 0.1 m
    - Mass: 1.0 kg

  Environment:
    - Gravity: [0, -9.81, 0]

  Initial conditions:
    - Pendulum starts horizontal
    - Bob positioned along +x from hinge
    - Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical
    - Initial angular velocity: 0 rad/s

  MuJoCo requirements:
    - Enable energy computation
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Joint rotates only about z-axis
    - Use SI units

  Acceptance criteria:
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Rod length = 0.2485 m
    - Rod mass = 0 kg
    - Bob mass = 1 kg
    - Gravity = [0, -9.81, 0]
    - Initial angle = +90 degrees
    - Initial angular velocity = 0
    - Energy computation enabled

output_format:
  type: xml
  constraints:
    - Return only valid MuJoCo XML
    - No explanations
    - No additional text

input.content:
<mujoco>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.01" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <joint name="bob_joint" type="free"/> <!-- This might conflict with the requirement of exactly one hinge joint. Let's rethink. -->
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>
</mujoco>

<mujoco>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.01" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" pos="0.2485 0 0" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
    </body>
  </worldbody>
</mujoco>

<mujoco>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Rod: Length 0.2485 m, Mass 0, Collision disabled -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.01" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <!-- Bob: Shape sphere, Diameter 0.1 m (radius 0.05), Mass 1.0 kg -->
      <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" pos="0.2485 0 0" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <!-- Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical (along +x) -->
    <!-- Since the joint is at [0, 1, 0] and rotates around Z, +90 degrees from vertical (y-axis) would be along x-axis -->
    <!-- The body is already at pos="0 1 0" and the geom is at pos="0.2485 0 0" relative to it. -->
    <!-- However, to specify initial state in MuJoCo, we typically use keyframes for pos/quat/mocap -->
    <!-- For a hinge joint, we can use qpos -->
    <key pos="0 1 0 1.5708 0 0 1" />
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>

<mujoco>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Rod: Length 0.2485 m, Mass 0, Collision disabled -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <!-- Bob: Shape sphere, Diameter 0.1 m (radius 0.05), Mass 1.0 kg -->
      <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" pos="0.2485 0 0" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <!-- 
      Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical.
      The joint is a hinge on the Z axis. 
      In MuJoCo, for a hinge joint on Z, the angle is typically around the Y axis.
      If vertical is 0, +90 degrees (horizontal) means rotating the rod from the Y axis to the X axis.
      However, the 'rod' geom is defined from (0,0,0) to (0.2485, 0, 0) in the body's local frame.
      This means the 'default' position is already horizontal.
      To have it start horizontal, the joint angle should be 0 if the local geom is horizontal.
      Wait, the instruction says: "Pendulum starts horizontal", "Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical".
      If the rod is at (0,0,0) to (0.2485, 0, 0), it IS horizontal in the local frame.
      So qpos for the hinge joint should be 0.
    -->
    <key qpos="1.5708" /> 
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>

<mujoco>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Rod: Length 0.2485 m, Mass 0, Collision disabled -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <!-- Bob: Shape sphere, Diameter 0.1 m (radius 0.05), Mass 1.0 kg -->
      <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" pos="0.2485 0 0" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <!-- 
    The instruction says: "Pendulum starts horizontal", "Bob positioned along +x from hinge".
    In the worldbody, the pendulum body is at (0, 1, 0).
    The rod goes from (0, 0, 0) to (0.2485, 0, 0) in local coordinates.
    This means the rod is already horizontal (along +x) in its local frame.
    Since the hinge joint axis is (0, 0, 1), the angle 0 corresponds to this horizontal position.
    If the vertical position was intended to be the 0 angle, the geom should have been along the y-axis.
    But the requirement "Bob positioned along +x from hinge" and "Pendulum starts horizontal" are consistent with the rod being along x.
    If +90 degrees from vertical is required, and vertical is 0, then qpos=1.5708 (pi/2) would be 90 degrees.
    However, if the '0' angle is already horizontal, then qpos=0 is the horizontal position.
    Let's re-read: "Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical".
    If vertical is Y axis (0, 1, 0), then +90 degrees from vertical (around Z) is +X axis (1, 0, 0).
    Our geom is already along +X. So qpos=0 should be the horizontal position.
    Wait, if the joint angle is 0, and the geom is at (0.2485, 0, 0), it's horizontal.
    If we want to follow "Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical", and we assume 0 is vertical, 
    then we should have the geom along Y and the qpos be 1.5708.
    BUT the instruction says "Bob positioned along +x from hinge".
    This implies the equilibrium (0 angle) might be vertical, but the starting position is horizontal.
    Let's make the rod vertical by default in the XML, then use a keyframe to rotate it to horizontal.
  -->
</mujoco>

<mujoco>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Rod: Length 0.2485 m, Mass 0, Collision disabled -->
      <!-- We define the rod along the Y axis so that 0 angle is vertical -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0 0.2485 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <!-- Bob: Shape sphere, Diameter 0.1 m (radius 0.05), Mass 1.0 kg -->
      <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" pos="0 0.2485 0" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <!-- 
      Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical (around Z axis, from Y to X).
      qpos for hinge joint: 1.5708 (pi/2)
      When qpos is 1.5708, the body rotates 90 deg around Z.
      Since it starts at Y, it will move to X.
      Position of bob in world: (0,1,0) + rotate((0,0.2485,0), 1.5708) = (0,1,0) + (0.2485,0,0) = (0.2485, 1, 0).
      This matches "Bob positioned along +x from hinge" (relative to hinge at 0,1,0).
    -->
    <key qpos="1.5708" />
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>

<mujoco>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Rod: Length 0.2485 m, Mass 0, Collision disabled -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0 0.2485 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <!-- Bob: Shape sphere, Diameter 0.1 m (radius 0.05), Mass 1.0 kg -->
      <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" pos="0 0.2485 0" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <!-- 
      Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical (around Z axis, from Y to X).
      qpos for hinge joint: 1.5708 (pi/2)
      When qpos is 1.5708, the body rotates 90 deg around Z.
      Since it starts at Y, it will move to X.
      Position of bob in world: (0,1,0) + rotate((0,0.2485,0), 1.5708) = (0,1,0) + (0.2485,0,0) = (0.2485, 1, 0).
      This matches "Bob positioned along +x from hinge" (relative to hinge at 0,1,0).
    -->
    <key qpos="1.5708" />
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>
